title: How to Sign Your First AI Automation Client (Without Starting an Agency)

extracted.hook: Most people who try to make money with AI start completely wrong.
transitions: So, in this video, I'll show you the exact step-by-step process to find, pitch, and sign your first AI automation client.
But real quick, before we dive into the step-by-step process, there are a few misconceptions that I need you to stop believing in.
And then myth number three is that you need a big audience.
So today, I'm showing you guys how to do that.
So really the takeaway here is just to not over complicate it, which most people do.
But to put this all in perspective, here's a quick analogy because you guys know I love analogies.
And once you start reaching out to people and pitching them, you're going to get objections and lots of questions.
So, now that we know who we're selling to.
Like it says on the cover of a hund00 million offers right here,
Let's not waste any time getting started.

analogies: "If you wanted to sell food, would you immediately just open a restaurant? Of course not. You'd probably start at home. You'd cook for a few friends or family members. Maybe even just do it for free to get some feedback."

"It's the same thing here, except of course, we're not selling food. We're actually selling outcomes. We're selling leverage. We're not specifically selling AI. The word AI gets thrown around a lot. It brings a lot of hype, but it can also bring a lot of hesitation."
extracted.vulnerability: "I've always said this, you don't start as an agency. You start as a freelancer. You evolve into a consultant. And only once you understand the full process, then you build that agency."

slow: "The truth is, converting a cold audience is really hard. Cold outreach is just a numbers game and when you're starting out, that can be discouraging."

frameworks: "The first step is to pick a niche that you either already understand or you can easily talk to."
